tool_args.label: No new facilities or sites determined
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA Agencies (MNR, LIRR, MTA Bus Company and NYCT) did not determine any new sites or locations of facilities such as storage facilities, maintenance facilities, operations centers, etc. during this reporting period, and does not have any new facilities planned for the next three-year period.

payload.claim_text: The MTA Agencies (MNR, LIRR, MTA Bus Company and NYCT) did not determine any new sites or locations of facilities such as storage facilities, maintenance facilities, operations centers, etc. during this reporting period, and does not have any new facilities planned for the next three-year period.
payload.description: No new facilities/sites determined during reporting period or planned for next three years

evidence_refs.source_quote: The MTA Agencies (MNR, LIRR, MTA Bus Company and NYCT) did not determine any new sites or locations of facilities such as storage facilities, maintenance facilities, operations centers, etc. during this reporting period, and does not have any new facilities planned for the next three-year period.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: LIRR monitoring - no significant differences found
tool_args.raw_text: As a summary of the Long Island Rail Road July 1, 2022 – June 30, 2025, Title VI associated quantitative analyses of the current service procedures (standards) the following assessment of compliance, predicated on the minority based comparisons is presented.

payload.claim_text: LIRR Service Standards and Policies Monitoring showed no significant difference between minority/non-minority groups for Service Availability, Vehicle Load, On-time Performance, and Vehicle Assignment. Vehicle Headway showed a statistically significant difference during off-peak (favorable to Low Income).
payload.description: LIRR Title VI service monitoring results for July 2022-June 2025

evidence_refs.source_quote: Service Standards and Policies Service Measure Standard Analysis Results – Minority Analysis Results – Poverty Analysis Notes/Mitigation Service Availability ... t-test No significant difference No significant difference ... Vehicle Headway ... t-test No significant difference Statistically significant difference during off-peak (Favorable to Low Income)

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: NYCT monitoring - no disparate impact found
tool_args.raw_text: Assessment of Compliance Monitoring Transit Service: System Standard or Policy Service Disparate Impact Vehicle Headway Subway None ... Vehicle Assignment Bus None

payload.claim_text: NYCT Title VI compliance assessment shows no disparate impact found across all monitored service standards and policies: Vehicle Headway (Subway and Bus), Vehicle Load (Subway and Bus), On-Time Performance (Subway and Bus), Service Availability (Subway and Bus), Transit Amenities (Subway), and Vehicle Assignment (Subway and Bus).
payload.description: NYCT systemwide monitoring shows no disparate impact for July 2022-June 2025

evidence_refs.source_quote: System Standard or Policy Service Disparate Impact Vehicle Headway Subway None Vehicle Headway Bus None Vehicle Load Subway None Vehicle Load Bus None On-Time Performance Subway None On-Time Performance Bus None Service Availability Subway None Service Availability Bus None Transit Amenities Subway None Transit Amenities Bus Not Applicable Vehicle Assignment Subway None Vehicle Assignment Bus None

tool_args.label: NYCT/MTA Bus Major Service Change definitions
tool_args.raw_text: NYCT and MTA Bus use the following definitions of 'major' service changes: • Route restructuring actions resulting in at least a 25% change in overall route length; • Service frequency changes resulting in at least a 25% change in annual revenue vehicle miles ('RVM') ... or • Span change actions resulting in at least one hour change in service span.

payload.claim_text: NYCT and MTA Bus use the following definitions of 'major' service changes: Route restructuring actions resulting in at least a 25% change in overall route length; Service frequency changes resulting in at least a 25% change in annual revenue vehicle miles (RVM); or Span change actions resulting in at least one hour change in service span.
payload.description: NYCT and MTA Bus Company Major Service Change definitions

evidence_refs.source_quote: Route restructuring actions resulting in at least a 25% change in overall route length; • Service frequency changes resulting in at least a 25% change in annual revenue vehicle miles ("RVM") Annual RVMs are a compilation of Weekday, Saturday and Sunday RVMs; or • Span change actions resulting in at least one hour change in service span.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
